reviewsSample.text: Qeyseri Bazaar is one of the most authentic and lively places in Erbil, full of history, culture, and local life. You can find traditional clothes, spices, sweets, gold shops, and many local products at reasonable prices. The atmosphere is unique and reflects the true spirit of the city. However, it can be crowded and a bit messy at times. Still, it’s a must-visit place to experience real Erbil.

Qaysari Bazaar is a must-see. For history, culture, local flavor, sensory richness, it’s hard to beat. Yes, malls are sleek and modern, but they don’t give you the same texture of place. Bring comfortable footwear, a spirit of curiosity, and maybe some bargaining skills.

It’s especially great if you like:
•	Traditional crafts and handmade goods
•	Sampling local food and sweets
•	People-watching and slow wandering rather than rushing
•	Places with history you can feel in the walls
An authentic and historic marketplace with everything that you can find from unique food to traditional garments this is the best place to visit in Erbil

A beautiful traditional souk/ bazar (shopping center) with many different shops and restaurants. People are so nice and helpful. Here you can buy traditional sweets, food, perfumes and clothes. The atmosphere is welcoming. I really loved being here.

reviewsSample.text:
Very bad service… not answering questions not giving information about patient 👎🏼
When we ask something the nurses say we will just do it and they never came back to us, not good experience with doctors they come very late to check the daily checkup. It’s been 4 nights we are there with our patient but we had a worse experience.

If you are a foreign i dont recommend this hospital at all.

We’ve contacted the secretary from JSK clinic (Nagham) to book an appointment with Dr.Hayman Hamdad. She gave us a price of 6500 dollars. Told us to come to the hospital at the same day of surgery and do the investigation and go through with the operation  on the same day ( calmed doctor approved of this). We booked tickets and went to see this doctor which he kept us waiting for 1.5 hours to see him!! Gave us a different price (5000 dollars).

When we tried to do labs, staff were asking for bribes indirectly!!! When we refused to pay they kept delaying us!!

We stopped contacting the secretary as she was obviously trying to robe us 1500 dollars, she kept sending messages threatening to cancel the operation.

On the day of surgery, they changes the price back to 6500, staff were rude. The doctor changed the price we agreed on to 7000$ !!  Staff told us that this doctor is the CEO of the hospital?!

We canceled everything and booked a ticket back home.

Very unprofessional unethical hospital. Worst experience ever.
Very bad hospital it's expensive the hospital it's not clean bad service

My husband was in severe pain early in the morning and I took him in to the Par Emergency room -before 9 am. We were one of only a few people there and were seen immediately. The staff was professional and helpful. I believe they had all labs finished, ultrasound completed, and a diagnosis within the first 45 minutes. Emergency surgery was scheduled a little later that day (2:30 pm). All went well. Doctors and nurses checked in several times. We were given the choice to stay overnight in a private room or go home, to which my husband chose home. We left around midnight.

For those who are foreign, the system may be a little different. Each step at the hospital you must pay for and if any medication is needed you must go outside to the Pharmacy -I made 7 trips to the accountant and 7 trips to the pharmacy. For example, as my husband was being wheeled back to surgery, I was asked to run to the pharmacy to get medication / IVs -I'm assuming which was used during surgery. This is important to note, as you may want to bring a friend or family member to assist you in the running -especially if you are in pain. I only mention this in case there are any single people who may read this, as I once lived as a single in this country. :)

My only complaint is I wish there had been some better pain killers for my husband afterward, but I think we figured it all out and he's all good now...

Oh and the parking is really difficult too once it gets busy midday!

Thanks, Par Hospital!

If i could i would give them zero stars! The worst possible hospital in Hawler, the staff are SO RUDE. You can’t even ask them basic questions in fear of them being rude! Imagine scheduling an operation for 11AM but then doing it at 4PM and then saying thats just how it is! And then they called us during the operation telling us in the middle of operation they have taken our patients to ICU and the doctor needs us ASAP, we ask why? Why ICU? Their ans is “idk” simply IDK. The fear they put the patient through getting them admitted to the hospital at 8AM promising the operation at 11 and then delaying it so much, the fear they put our family through! And still being treated rudely, regardless of the bag of money they take!!

It is a place to buy books. The majority of the books found there are in English, however, Kurdish, Arabic, and Persian books are also offered. Books for every levels and of every kind are presented there. For example, you can find novels, short stories, thrillers, literary fiction, historical fiction, science fiction (Sci-Fi), romance, literature, poetry, history, detective and mystery, action and adventure, science, economics, politics, memoir, biographies and autobiographies, and self-help or guide, and comics as well as religious. Haval Bookstore is a quiet and tidy place. You will feel really comfortable. The staffers working there are respectful, courteous and well-behaved. They will help you out with finding your interested book or books. Moreover, you can also find stationery services such as cut paper, envelopes, writing implements, continuous form paper, and other office supplies easily, and that is owing to the fact that Haval Bookstore is a part of Haval Printing Press Company which provides stationery services, e.g., stationery equipment, printing, and photographing. More than that, it is one of the best places to order books and items of your interest. You can order your interested items online by making a phone call with the kindly-talking telephone-line staff, and you will receive your ordered item in the soonest time.

Sheikhallah Bazaar
.
This bazaar is 700 meters long, situated east of the Erbil citadel. It is one of the most famous and dynamic markets of Erbil. Sheikh Allah Market
A popular market where fruits and vegetables are very cheap.
Groceries are also cheap, but pay attention to the expiration dates, as some items are expired, spoiled, or have been exposed to a lot of sun.

Also, be careful with other items, such as mobile phone accessories; some may be unusable.

Finally, be careful while walking, as the place is very crowded.
